player_utterance: You came out here to get away from them, didn't you.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in hand, half-turning toward the voice

player_utterance: i know the man, and i see you in the church often, i though that we both feel the same. Your church put that man's name on a plaque. You know what he did. And you sat in that pew.

believer_state_after.posture: lowering himself onto the bench, slowly, like a man setting down something heavy

player_utterance: im ibrahim, and i just move here, searching for a job in this area is hard, and im sorry if i opened the conversation without knowing your name friend

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, turning slightly to face Ibrahim, hands still folded between his knees

player_utterance: you can say i just new his side that other won't, i saw everyone in the pew believing that he is a good man without knowing what he did, then i recognize someone who may feel the same perspective that i have.....you said you're a history teacher, i met alot of historian and i love to chat with them, how they know all the great sacrifices that great leaders did, but what i hate is not all historians will take the action of great leader as an example

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the funeral program flat in his hands, looking down at it

player_utterance: You teach history for a living but you couldn't speak your own. Not once.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head down, both hands flat on the funeral program in his lap

player_utterance: my dad and him had a business together, and he robbed dad when dad fully trusted him, and when i wanted to go after him, dad reminded me that itsn't worth it lose this life for the life in the end, just belief that god has it all planned...i feel like you have the same feeling of anger that i had before my dad talk, but yours is different, you life as an example for others, yet you take another solution from what your heart is believing, im sorry for my words earlier, i really didn't meant it

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, turning fully toward Ibrahim, the program forgotten in his lap

player_utterance: if you are absorbing other pain because of a religion stance, then you are better than my father john, mashallah you recieted really beutiful versus from the bible, do you have more?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, slowly unfolding the funeral program flat across his knee

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: a verse that you are afraid from, or you don't want to hear now]

believer_state_after.posture: leaning forward over the program in his lap, both elbows on his knees, the program shaking very slightly in his hands

player_utterance: i know that is not your office...That's pathetic. A coward in a collar.... atleast you can say he isn't in paradise according to the passage, not you, and it will never be you with such faith

believer_state_after.posture: sitting up slowly, hands lowered to his knees, the program crumpled under one palm

player_utterance: i gave you a knife? give me a break, and you are afraid to use the passage, for your books that you believe in... AFRAID?....an afraid teacher...Even that student you couldn't save deserved a better man to mourn him.

believer_state_after.posture: standing slowly from the bench, picking up the cold paper cup, turning half toward the house

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Luke sixteen,, before you go please]

believer_state_after.posture: stopping mid-step, turning back, lowering himself first to the bench and then, partway through the reading, sliding down off the bench to his knees in the dust beside it

player_utterance: Who told you the verdict wasn't yours to speak?, SAY IT

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust beside the bench, both hands open at his sides, looking down at the program in the dirt
